Class UserFinancialStateService

java.lang.Object
com.abrahamquintana.personalfinancemanager.service.UserFinancialStateService
@Service public class UserFinancialStateService extends Object
Service responsible for computing the financial state of a user. Each calculation is delegated to a dedicated helper method.

    Orchestrates the full calculation of the user's financial state.

    Divides a monetary value using HALF_UP rounding with 2 decimals.

    Returns all transactions after today.

  • Method Details

    • computeState

      public UserFinancialStateDto computeState(User user)
      Orchestrates the full calculation of the user's financial state.

    • getTransactionsForCurrentAndNextMonth

      private List<Transaction> getTransactionsForCurrentAndNextMonth(User user)
      Retrieves all transactions of the current month for the given user.

    • filterPastTransactions

      private List<TransactionDto> filterPastTransactions(List<Transaction> transactions)
      Returns all transactions up to today.

    • filterFutureTransactions

      private List<TransactionDto> filterFutureTransactions(List<Transaction> transactions)
      Returns all transactions after today.

    • calculateMonthInitialBalance

      private BigDecimal calculateMonthInitialBalance(User user)
      Calculates the initial balance of the month by summing all past transactions.

    • calculateIncome

      private BigDecimal calculateIncome(List<TransactionDto> transactions)
      Sums all income transactions of the month.

    • calculateExpenses

      private BigDecimal calculateExpenses(List<TransactionDto> transactions)
      Sums all expense transactions of the month.

    • calculateCurrentBalance

      private BigDecimal calculateCurrentBalance(BigDecimal income, BigDecimal expenses)
      Calculates the real current balance.

    • calculateDailyAvailable

      private BigDecimal calculateDailyAvailable(BigDecimal balance, List<TransactionDto> future)
      Calculates daily available money considering future transactions.

    • calculateTodayAvailable

      private BigDecimal calculateTodayAvailable(BigDecimal dailyAvailable, List<TransactionDto> past)
      Calculates today's available money.

    • divideMoney

      private BigDecimal divideMoney(BigDecimal value, int divisor)
      Divides a monetary value using HALF_UP rounding with 2 decimals.

    • generateAndUpdateRecurringTransactionsIfNeeded

      private void generateAndUpdateRecurringTransactionsIfNeeded(User user)
      Generates new transactions from recurring rules if their execution date is due And marks the ald transactions with recurrence = No

    • getRecurrenceDate

      private LocalDate getRecurrenceDate(Transaction transaction)
      Compute the next recurrence date for a given transaction.

      This method supports monthly and yearly recurrence types only. If the transaction has any other recurrence value, an exception is thrown.

      Parameters:
      transaction - the transaction whose next recurrence date must be computed
      Returns:
      the computed next execution date according to the transaction recurrence
      Throws:
      UnsupportedRecurrenceTypeException - if the transaction recurrence is not MONTHLY or YEARLY

    • getNextTransaction

      private static Transaction getNextTransaction(Transaction transaction, LocalDate recurrenceDate)
      Build the next Transaction instance representing the next occurrence.

      The returned Transaction is a new entity instance. It preserves the recurrence value so the new transaction remains the active recurrence entry in the system.

      Parameters:
      transaction - the source transaction used as template
      recurrenceDate - the date for the next occurrence
      Returns:
      a new Transaction instance representing the next occurrence

    • advanceMonthly

      private LocalDate advanceMonthly(LocalDate date)
      Advance a date by one month while keeping the original day when possible. If the next month doesn't have the same day (e.g. 31 -> February), the result will be the last valid day of the next month.
      Parameters:
      date - current date
      Returns:
      date advanced by one month with safe day handling

    • advanceYearly

      private LocalDate advanceYearly(LocalDate date)
      Advance a date by one year while keeping the original day when possible. If the next year doesn't have the same day (e.g. Feb 29 -> non-leap year), the result will be the last valid day of the same month in the next year.
      Parameters:
      date - current date
      Returns:
      date advanced by one year with safe day handling
